basics.name: Rana A. Barghout
basics.label: PhD student
basics.summary: Hello, I’m Rana, a PhD student at the University of Toronto, currently immersed in the world of enzyme modeling and design in the context of metabolic networks. My research revolves around combining machine learning with biochemical engineering to enhance genome‑scale modeling and computational protein engineering. I’m passionate about pushing the boundaries of metabolic engineering by leveraging deep learning models to predict kinetic parameters and adapt genome‑scale models in large‑scale optimization frameworks.

education.description: Research in metabolic engineering and machine learning. Focused on integrating deep learning techniques with biochemical models to improve enzyme design and genome‑scale metabolic simulations.

education.degree: Bachelor of Applied Science in Chemical and Biological Engineering
education.description: Specialized in chemical and biological engineering with honours. Developed a strong foundation in biochemical processes, data analysis and engineering design.

volunteer.summary: Work on streamlining the volunteer recruitment process and supporting programs for under‑represented and underserved youth across Toronto.
Assist the society’s president with administrative tasks, plan events and build connections across Toronto centred around entrepreneurship, charity and professional development.
Coordinated mentorship groups for incoming graduate students in the department, pairing them with senior graduate students and organizing events to facilitate community building.

awards.title: Rein Otson Memorial Graduate Scholarship
awards.awarder: University of Toronto Department of Chemical Engineering & Applied Chemistry
awards.summary: Awarded to outstanding international students on the basis of academic merit.

awards.title: MAX Scholarship in Science and Engineering
awards.summary: Awarded to science and engineering students demonstrating high academic achievement and contribution to the community.

awards.title: UBC International Community Achievement Award
awards.summary: Recognized for leadership in community service, international engagement and promotion of diversity.

publications.title: Advances in Generative Modeling Methods and Datasets to Design Novel Enzymes for Renewable Chemicals and Fuels
publications.authors: Rana A. Barghout, Zhiqing Xu, Siddharth Betala, Radhakrishnan Mahadevan
publications.summary: This review summarizes state‑of‑the‑art generative models for protein design and how they can be applied to bioenergy applications. It discusses underlying architectures, training strategies and the importance of high‑quality datasets for training and evaluating generative models. The paper also highlights the potential of applying generative models to strain design for bioenergy production.

publications.title: KinMod database: a tool for investigating metabolic regulation
publications.authors: Kiandokht Haddadi, Rana A. Barghout, Radhakrishnan Mahadevan
publications.summary: This article introduces the KinMod database, which catalogs over two million data points on enzyme regulation across 9,814 organisms. KinMod employs a hierarchical data structure to map kinetic parameters to proteins and regulatory interactions, facilitating machine learning approaches for parameter estimation and enabling comprehensive metabolic network analysis.

publications.title: Foundations of Chemical and Biological Engineering I
publications.authors: Jonathan Verret, Rosie Qiao, Rana A. Barghout
publications.summary: A workbook developed to accompany the Foundations of Chemical and Biological Engineering I course, providing students with practice problems and step‑by‑step explanations.

projects.name: KinMod: Metabolism Regulation Database
projects.description: An open‑access database created to catalogue and analyze regulatory interactions in metabolic networks. It aggregates enzyme regulation data across various organisms and provides customizable search options for researchers.

projects.name: Compound‑Protein Interaction Prediction
projects.description: A deep learning model that predicts compound‑protein interactions using improved embedding techniques and transformer‑inspired methods. The project focuses on enhancing prediction accuracy and interpretability for drug discovery applications.

projects.name: kinGEMs: Enzyme‑Constrained Genome‑Scale Models Pipeline
projects.description: A computational pipeline for constructing enzyme‑constrained genome‑scale metabolic models using deep learning predicted kinetic parameters. It automates model construction and provides tools for analyzing metabolic capabilities.

projects.name: Explorations in Deep Learning
projects.description: A collection of deep learning experiments covering computer vision, generative models, natural language processing and drug discovery. These projects explore cutting‑edge neural network architectures and training techniques.

blogPosts.title: Using AI to Accelerate Metabolic Engineering
blogPosts.summary: A look into how artificial intelligence can streamline the design of metabolic pathways, with examples from recent research.

blogPosts.title: Deep Learning for Protein Design: An Introduction
blogPosts.summary: An accessible overview of deep learning approaches to protein design and why these methods are revolutionizing biotechnology.

blogPosts.title: Building Community Through STEM Outreach
blogPosts.summary: Reflections on volunteering with BridgeTO Youth and the importance of mentorship in science, technology, engineering and mathematics.
